# Payment Module Documentation

> PLEASE NOTE!
>
> The keys shown in this guide **DO NOT GRANT ACCESS TO THE FUNCTIONALITY**, but they do let you verify that signature
> generation works correctly in your application.
>
> To work with the API you must be registered in the service.
>

## General information

* All time-related fields are specified with zero offset (UTC+0) in the *YYYY-MM-DD'T'hh:mm:ss* format.<br>**Example:**
  2023-08-20T13:51:00 (Moscow time: 2023-08-20T16:51:00)
* API keys are **case-sensitive**.
* **The API is not localised.** Text generated by the server — error `message` values, and `name` fields of reference
  data such as currencies and payment methods — is returned in Russian. The examples below reproduce real requests and
  responses verbatim, so Russian strings appear inside them by design; an English gloss is given next to them where it
  helps. Integrate against the `errorType` and `status` codes, not against message text.

## HTTP return codes

| Return code | Description                                                                                                                                                                   |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 200         | The operation completed successfully, a JSON object is returned                                                                                                                |
| 4XX         | Used for malformed requests; the problem is on the sender's side                                                                                                               |
| 401         | Used for authorization errors                                                                                                                                                  |
| 403         | Used for access errors                                                                                                                                                         |
| 5XX         | Used for internal errors; the problem is on the payment system's side. **Important: do NOT treat this as a failed operation; the execution state is UNKNOWN and the operation may have succeeded** |

## Authorization

* **Mandatory:** every request, REGARDLESS OF ITS TYPE, must carry a timestamp (the current time in UTC), passed in the
  `timestamp` query parameter.

> GET /api/orders?timestamp=2023-08-20T13:51:00

To authorize a request, place the following parameters in the request header:

| Header    | Content                    |
|-----------|----------------------------|
| UID       | **Personal key (UID)**     |
| SIGNATURE | **MD5 hash (SIGNATURE)**   |

* **The headers must be generated for every API request**
* **The MD5 hash is built differently depending on the request type (GET/POST)**
* Computing the **MD5 hash (SIGNATURE)** requires the **personal key (UID)** and the **private key (PRIVATE_KEY)**. Both
  can be obtained in the merchant dashboard.
* **ATTENTION! PRIVATE_KEY IS USED ONLY TO SIGN THE REQUEST AND IS NEVER TRANSMITTED IN PLAIN FORM!**
  <br>

> The signature examples below are byte-exact and are meant for verifying your implementation. Do not retype or
> translate anything inside them — any change to the payload changes the resulting MD5.

### GET request

| Parameter   | Description                                                            | Example                                                   |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| UID         | Personal key                                                           | <code>e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3</code>         |
| PRIVATE_KEY | Private key                                                            | <code>GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=</code> |
| PARAMS      | The concatenation of all parameters in the "param1=a&param2=b" format  | <code>timestamp=2023-08-20T13:51:00&page=2</code>         |

The signature is MD5-HASH("{UID}:{PRIVATE_KEY}:{PARAMS}")

Example signature body for a GET request:

    e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3:GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=:timestamp=2024-05-04T12:14:07&pageNumber=0&pageSize=4

The resulting md5 signature

    6c5711fa920e460e88dee24e0e5d61cd

### POST request

| Parameter   | Description       | Example                                                                                                                                            |
|-------------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| UID         | Personal key      | <code>e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3</code>                                                                                                  |
| PRIVATE_KEY | Private key       | <code>GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=</code>                                                                                          |
| PAYLOAD     | The request body  | <code>{"OrderId": "11111111232132","Amount": 10000,"CallbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result","ReturnUrl":"https://site.com/pay/success"}</code> |

The signature is MD5-HASH("{UID}:{PRIVATE_KEY}:{PAYLOAD}")

Example signature body for a POST request:

```
    e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3:GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=:{
    "externalId": "111111112321341",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "USD",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
    "clientInfo": "User777"
}
```

The resulting md5 signature

    d56e3c322c0cbb51897593085f0fcf99

All methods can be provided as a Postman collection, which you need to import and then configure the variables for

| Variable    | Description                 | Example                                                   |
|-------------|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| UID         | Personal key                | <code>e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3</code>         |
| PRIVATE_KEY | Private key                 | <code>GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=</code> |
| api_server  | Integration service URL     | <code>http://url-payment-sercive/api</code>               |

The collection has a signing script defined for every request, so you can cross-check against the generated values if
needed

### Multipart request (form-data)

Used for requests with attached files (for example, creating a dispute — `POST /dispute`).

| Parameter       | Description                                              | Example                                                         |
|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| UID             | Personal key                                             | <code>e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3</code>               |
| PRIVATE_KEY     | Private key                                              | <code>GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=</code>       |
| JSON_PART       | The content of the request's `json` part (UTF-8 string)  | <code>{"hash":"a7efd403-...","comment":"Платеж выполнен"}</code> |
| FILE_1, FILE_2… | The request's file parts (each file is a separate part)  | binary file data                                                |

**PAYLOAD construction algorithm:**

1. Take the content of the part named `json` as a UTF-8 string.
2. For each file (parts where `submittedFileName != null`) compute the MD5 of its bytes — the result is lowercase hex (32 characters).
3. Concatenate in transmission order: `PAYLOAD = json_content + md5hex(file1) + md5hex(file2) + ...`

The signature is MD5-HASH("{UID}:{PRIVATE_KEY}:{PAYLOAD}")

Example for a request with a single file (the `comment` value is Russian for "Payment completed" — it is shown verbatim
because the signature is computed over the exact bytes you send):

```
json part: {"hash":"a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6","comment":"Платеж выполнен","callbackUrl":"https://site.com/dispute/callback","orderType":"IN"}
file MD5:  d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e

Signature body:
e7742caf-5e74-498c-8f4f-d4ae0a6f2bf3:GJI/1MP8MvE/ODbnplDlnaQb8ZYQb8ZYIVSg+II6xnA=:{"hash":"a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6","comment":"Платеж выполнен","callbackUrl":"https://site.com/dispute/callback","orderType":"IN"}d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e
```

> ATTENTION! The order of files in a multipart request MATTERS — the file MD5 hashes are concatenated strictly in the order in which the parts are transmitted in the request. Reordering the files produces a different signature and an authorization error.

---

## Payment/balance methods

### Get balance

`GET /balance`

Example request:
`/api/balance?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:30:07`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "amount": 1251.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
}
```

### Get available payment methods

`GET /payment_method_definitions`

Example request:
`/api/payment_method_definitions?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:30:07`

Response (status code 200) — `"Банк США"` is "US Bank" and `"Доллар США"` is "US Dollar"; reference-data names are
returned in Russian:

```
{
    "success": true,
    "result": [
        {
            "id": "d53e3d2e-4f9e-4570-ad5c-0b7647e45e48",
            "name": "Банк США",
            "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER",
            "currency": {
                "code": "USD",
                "name": "Доллар США",
                "isActive": true,
                "precision": 2
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

## Currency conversion methods

### Get currency conversion method information by conversion method identifier

`GET /conversion_method/{methodCode}`

Example request:
`/conversion_method/usd_to_rub?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:30:07`

Response (status code 200):

```
{
    "id": "usd_to_rub",
    "currencyCodeFrom": "USD",
    "currencyCodeTo": "RUB",
    "conversionFactor": 0.0110000000
}
```
The response returns information about the currency conversion method.

* `id` — the conversion method identifier
* `currencyCodeFrom` — the code of the currency being converted from
* `currencyCodeTo` — the code of the currency being converted to
* `conversionFactor` — the conversion factor

## Methods for working with pay-in orders

### Create a fiat pay-in order

`POST /order`

* You can optionally pass `paymentMethodDefinitionId` to create the order with a specific payment method.

* Providing client information in the "ClientInfo" field is recommended.

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter                 | Type    | Required | Notes                                                            |
|---------------------------|---------|----------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| externalId                | string  | Yes      | External order key                                               |
| amount                    | decimal | Yes      | Order amount                                                     |
| currency                  | string  | Yes      | Order currency code                                              |
| callbackUrl               | string  | No       | Notification URL                                                 |
| clientInfo                | string  | No       | Client information                                               |
| paymentMethodDefinitionId | string  | No       | Payment method identifier                                        |
| type                      | string  | No       | Payment method type                                              |
| conversionMethod          | string  | No       | Currency conversion method identifier (for cross-currency orders) |

The payment method type can take the following values

```
BANK_CARD_TRANSFER - transfer to a bank card
TRANSFER_BY_NUMBER - transfer by phone number
```

Example request:
`/api/order?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Creating an order

```<json>   
{
    "externalId": "111111112321341",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "USD",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
    "clientInfo": "User777",
    "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER"
}
```

#### Creating an order with a chosen payment method `paymentMethodDefinitionId`

```<json>
{
  "externalId": "11111111232132",
  "amount": 10.5,
  "currency": "RUB",
  "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
  "clientInfo": "User777",
  "paymentMethodDefinitionId": "55422a79-59f7-4bab-b3dc-1b2242c2dac7"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
        "externalId": "111111112321341",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "merchantAmount": 95.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "details": {
            "phoneNumber": "1",
            "cardNumber": "2",
            "qr": null,
            "ussd": null,
            "cardHolder": null,
            "secretCode": null
        },
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "paymentMethod": {
            "id": "d53e3d2e-4f9e-4570-ad5c-0b7647e45e48",
            "name": "Банк США",
            "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER",
            "currency": {
                "code": "USD",
                "name": "Доллар США",
                "isActive": true,
                "precision": 2
            }
        },
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T15:34:55.56096812+03:00",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

<span id="order_description">Order description</span>

The response returns information about the order.

* `hash` — the identifier assigned to the order in our system
* `externalId` — the identifier passed by the external system
* `currency` — the order currency code
* `details` — payment details information. Depending on the payment type it will contain a card number `cardNumber`
  and/or a phone number `phoneNumber` to send the payment to. Additionally the following may be filled in: `qr` (QR
  code), `ussd` (USSD command), `cardHolder` (cardholder), `secretCode` (secret code)
* `paymentMethod` — information about the assigned payment method
* `status` — the order status
* `closeReasonCode` — the order close reason code (filled in when the order is finalised)
* `userCurrency` — the user's settlement currency, the one their balance is held in (filled in for cross-currency orders)
* `exchangeRate` — the factor for converting the user's currency into the order currency
* `secondAmount` — the order amount in the user's currency

the status can be

```
PENDING - in progress
SUCCESS - successful,
CANCELED - rejected,
EXPIRED - expired
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the messages read "Currency not found in the system" and "Payment method not found in the
system":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Валюта не найдена в системе"
        },
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Метод оплаты не найден в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Failed to allocate payment details":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "FAILED_SELECT_PAYMENT_METHOD",
            "message": "Не удалось выделить реквизит"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Confirm a fiat order

`POST /order/{hash}/confirm`

* Used when the user has paid the order to the given payment details and confirms it

* This parameter is not required for the system to function. It serves for informational purposes only

* The request body is empty

* The `hash` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/order/a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6/confirm?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200):

```<json>   
{
    "success": true,
    "result": "OK"
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order has already been confirmed by the client":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка уже подтверждена клиентом"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get a fiat order by hash

`GET /order/{hash}`

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `hash` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/order/a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "71ac21c7-d65b-47dd-89ad-17a441cb7838",
        "externalId": "111111112321344",
        "amount": 100.0000000000,
        "merchantAmount": 95.0000000000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "details": {
            "phoneNumber": "1",
            "cardNumber": "2",
            "qr": null,
            "ussd": null,
            "cardHolder": null,
            "secretCode": null
        },
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "paymentMethod": {
            "id": "d53e3d2e-4f9e-4570-ad5c-0b7647e45e48",
            "name": "Банк США",
            "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER",
            "currency": {
                "code": "USD",
                "name": "Доллар США",
                "isActive": true,
                "precision": 2
            }
        },
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T13:32:06.505575Z",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

See the [Order description](#order_description) above

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by hash":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по хэшу не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get a fiat order by external key

`GET /order/ext/{externalId}`

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `externalId` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/order/ext/111111112321344?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:43:28`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "71ac21c7-d65b-47dd-89ad-17a441cb7838",
        "externalId": "111111112321344",
        "amount": 100.0000000000,
        "merchantAmount": 95.0000000000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "details": {
            "phoneNumber": "1",
            "cardNumber": "2",
            "qr": null,
            "ussd": null,
            "cardHolder": null,
            "secretCode": null
        },
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "paymentMethod": {
            "id": "d53e3d2e-4f9e-4570-ad5c-0b7647e45e48",
            "name": "Банк США",
            "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER",
            "currency": {
                "code": "USD",
                "name": "Доллар США",
                "isActive": true,
                "precision": 2
            }
        },
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T13:32:06.505575Z",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

See the [Order description](#order_description) above

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by external ID":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по внешнему ID не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get fiat orders by filter

`GET /order`

* All filters are specified in the query parameters
* `timestamp` is the only required parameter
* This method supports pagination. The `pageNumber` and `pageSize` parameters control it
* This method supports sorting together with pagination. The `fieldNameSort` and `isDescSort` parameters control it
* On success, orders are returned in the following format:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "result": {
    "elements": [
      ...
    ],
    "totalElements": 2,
    "totalPages": 1,
    "currentPages": 0
  }
}
```

| Property      | Description                                                     |
|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| elements      | the array of orders described [above](#order_description)        |
| totalElements | the total number of elements found                              |
| totalPages    | the total number of pages of `pageSize` size                    |
| currentPages  | the current page                                                |

Query parameters

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

| Parameter     | Type     | Required | Notes                                             |
|---------------|----------|----------|---------------------------------------------------|
| timestamp     | DateTime | Yes      | Timestamp                                         |
| hash          | string   | No       | Filter by order hash                              |
| amountMin     | decimal  | No       | Lower amount bound filter                         |
| amountMax     | decimal  | No       | Upper amount bound filter                         |
| currency      | string[] | No       | Filter by currency (several values can be passed) |
| createdAfter  | DateTime | No       | Lower creation date bound filter                  |
| createdBefore | DateTime | No       | Upper creation date bound filter                  |
| orderStatus   | string   | No       | Order status                                      |
| pageNumber    | int      | No       | Page number                                       |
| pageSize      | int      | No       | Page size                                         |
| fieldNameSort | string   | No       | Sort field                                        |
| isDescSort    | boolean  | No       | Sort direction                                    |

`orderStatus` can be multi-valued — you can pass several statuses, for example `CANCELED` and `EXPIRED` — and it accepts
the values

```
PENDING - in progress
SUCCESS - successful,
CANCELED - rejected,
EXPIRED - expired
```

Example request:
`/api/order?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:55:11&orderStatus=PENDING&pageNumber=1&pageSize=10&fieldNameSort=externalId`

Fetches orders 11 through 20 (`pageNumber=1&pageSize=10`) that are unprocessed (`orderStatus=PENDING`), sorted by the
external order key (`fieldNameSort=externalId`)

## Notifications

We operate a notification system that notifies your service when a transaction status changes.

There are 10 notification attempts, repeated at a certain time interval.

Once you have received the status, you must return code 200 so that the system stops sending notifications for that
order.

### How does the callback work?

First of all, when creating an order you receive a HASH identifier, which holds the identifier in our system.

When the transaction status changes, you will receive callbacks at the address specified when the order was created.

If a callback address was specified in the merchant dashboard, then when `callbackUrl` is passed at order creation the
call will be made to the address passed in `callbackUrl`; if `callbackUrl` was not passed, the value from the merchant
dashboard is used. If no callback address can be found anywhere, no notifications are sent when the order status changes

A **POST** request is sent to the resolved address with the order body, in the format described [above](#order_description)

example:

```json
{
  "hash": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
  "externalId": "111111112321341",
  "amount": 100.00,
  "merchantAmount": 95.00,
  "details": {
    "phoneNumber": "1",
    "cardNumber": "2"
  },
  "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
  "clientInfo": "User777",
  "paymentMethod": {
    "id": "d53e3d2e-4f9e-4570-ad5c-0b7647e45e48",
    "name": "Банк США",
    "type": "BANK_CARD_TRANSFER",
    "currency": {
      "code": "USD",
      "name": "Доллар США",
      "isActive": true,
      "precision": 2
    }
  },
  "createdAt": "2024-05-04T15:34:55.56096812+03:00",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "exchangeRate": 0.588,
  "secondAmount": 58.79,
  "userCurrency": "USD"
}
```

The request is signed with the api key and the secret, analogously to the authorization used for calling the api methods

To verify that the information is correct, you **must** check the hash of the model against the hash from the "SIGNATURE"
header

### Verifying the callback signature

When a callback request is received, the service sends the following HTTP headers:

| Header    | Content                          |
|-----------|----------------------------------|
| UID       | The merchant's personal key      |
| SIGNATURE | The MD5 signature of the body    |

**Signature formula:**

```
SIGNATURE = MD5("{UID}:{PRIVATE_KEY}:{JSON_BODY}")
```

- `UID` — the same personal key used for signing API requests
- `PRIVATE_KEY` — the same private key (as in the "Authorization" section)
- `JSON_BODY` — the full body of the POST request (the JSON string exactly as it was received)

**Step-by-step verification algorithm:**

1. Read the `UID` header value
2. Read the `SIGNATURE` header value
3. Read the POST request body as a string (the raw JSON)
4. Compute `expected = MD5("{UID}:{PRIVATE_KEY}:{JSON_BODY}")`
5. Compare `expected` with the received `SIGNATURE` — if they match, the request is authentic

**Verification pseudocode:**

```
// A callback POST request has been received
uid = headers["UID"]
signature = headers["SIGNATURE"]
body = readRequestBody()  // the raw request body (JSON string)

expected = MD5(uid + ":" + PRIVATE_KEY + ":" + body)

if (expected == signature) {
    // The signature is valid — process the callback
    return 200
} else {
    // The signature does not match — reject
    return 403
}
```

> NOTE: This verification mechanism is identical for callback notifications about regular orders and about disputes.

Order status options:

```
PENDING - in progress
SUCCESS - successful,
CANCELED - rejected,
EXPIRED - expired
```
```
userCurrency - the user's settlement currency, the one their balance is held in
exchangeRate - the factor for converting the user's currency into the order currency
secondAmount - the order amount in the user's currency
```

## Payout order methods

### Create a payout order

`POST /payout`

* Providing information about the payout method (bank name, e-wallet, etc.) in the "payoutMethod" field is recommended —
  (a string).

* On success, information about the payout is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `payoutExpirationDate` parameter must be sent in the MM/YY format

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter            | Type    | Required | Notes                                                                                     |
|----------------------|---------|----------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| externalId           | string  | Yes      | External payout key                                                                       |
| amount               | decimal | Yes      | Payout amount                                                                             |
| currency             | string  | Yes      | Payout currency code                                                                      |
| callbackUrl          | string  | No       | Notification URL                                                                          |
| clientInfo           | string  | No       | Client information                                                                        |
| payoutMethod         | string  | No       | Information about the payout method (bank name, etc.)                                     |
| payoutInfo           | string  | Yes      | Payout details (card number, phone number, etc.)                                          |
| payoutExpirationDate | string  | No       | Card expiration date (required when the payout method needs this value). Format `MM/YY`   |

Example request:
`/api/payout?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Creating a payout

`payoutMethod` is free-form text naming the payout method; it is passed through to the operator who fulfils the payout:

```<json>   
{
    "externalId": "102",
    "amount": 10,
    "currency": "USD",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
    "payoutMethod": "Tinkoff",
    "payoutInfo": "4644 4654 4546 3133",
    "payoutExpirationDate": "05/29"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "8d71eb70-6e4c-48dd-b32c-99db11907ee3",
        "externalId": "102",
        "amount": 10.00,
        "merchantAmount": 10.50,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "createdAt": "2024-05-17T16:09:29.492512093+03:00",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

The response returns information about the payout.

* `hash` — the identifier assigned to the payout in our system
* `externalId` — the identifier passed by the external system
* `status` — the payout status
* `closeReasonCode` — the payout close reason code (filled in when the order is finalised)
* `userCurrency` — the user's settlement currency (filled in for cross-currency payouts)
* `exchangeRate` — the factor for converting the user's currency into the payout currency
* `secondAmount` — the payout amount in the user's currency

the status can be

```
PENDING - in progress
SUCCESS - successful,
CANCELED - rejected,
EXPIRED - expired
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Currency not found in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Валюта не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Failed to allocate payment details":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "FAILED_SELECT_PAYMENT_METHOD",
            "message": "Не удалось выделить реквизит"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get a payout by hash

`GET /payout/{hash}`

* On success, information about the payout is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `hash` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/payout/a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "8d71eb70-6e4c-48dd-b32c-99db11907ee3",
        "externalId": "102",
        "amount": 10.00,
        "merchantAmount": 10.50,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "createdAt": "2024-05-17T16:09:29.492512093+03:00",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by hash":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по хэшу не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get a payout by external key

`GET /payout/ext/{externalId}`

* On success, information about the payout is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `externalId` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/payout/ext/111111112321344?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:43:28`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "8d71eb70-6e4c-48dd-b32c-99db11907ee3",
        "externalId": "102",
        "amount": 10.00,
        "merchantAmount": 10.50,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "createdAt": "2024-05-17T16:09:29.492512093+03:00",
        "status": "PENDING",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by external ID":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по внешнему ID не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get payouts by filter

`GET /payout`

* All filters are specified in the query parameters
* `timestamp` is the only required parameter
* This method supports pagination. The `pageNumber` and `pageSize` parameters control it
* This method supports sorting together with pagination. The `fieldNameSort` and `isDescSort` parameters control it
* On success, payouts are returned in the following format:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "result": {
    "elements": [
      ...
    ],
    "totalElements": 2,
    "totalPages": 1,
    "currentPages": 0
  }
}
```

| Property      | Description                                  |
|---------------|----------------------------------------------|
| elements      | the array of payouts described above         |
| totalElements | the total number of elements found           |
| totalPages    | the total number of pages of `pageSize` size |
| currentPages  | the current page                             |

Query parameters

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

| Parameter     | Type     | Required | Notes                                             |
|---------------|----------|----------|---------------------------------------------------|
| timestamp     | DateTime | Yes      | Timestamp                                         |
| hash          | string   | No       | Filter by payout hash                             |
| amountMin     | decimal  | No       | Lower amount bound filter                         |
| amountMax     | decimal  | No       | Upper amount bound filter                         |
| currency      | string[] | No       | Filter by currency (several values can be passed) |
| createdAfter  | DateTime | No       | Lower creation date bound filter                  |
| createdBefore | DateTime | No       | Upper creation date bound filter                  |
| orderStatus   | string   | No       | Payout status                                     |
| pageNumber    | int      | No       | Page number                                       |
| pageSize      | int      | No       | Page size                                         |
| fieldNameSort | string   | No       | Sort field                                        |
| isDescSort    | boolean  | No       | Sort direction                                    |

`orderStatus` can be multi-valued — you can pass several statuses, for example `CANCELED` and `EXPIRED` — and it accepts
the values

```
PENDING - in progress
SUCCESS - successful,
CANCELED - rejected,
EXPIRED - expired
```

Example request:
`/api/payout?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:55:11&orderStatus=PENDING&pageNumber=1&pageSize=10&fieldNameSort=externalId`

Fetches payouts 11 through 20 (`pageNumber=1&pageSize=10`) that are unprocessed (`orderStatus=PENDING`), sorted by the
external payout key (`fieldNameSort=externalId`)

-----------------------------------------------------

## Acquiring

Acquiring is a pay-in order (PAY IN) with the following steps:

* The client (player) enters their card details
* The BNN system creates an order to debit the client's funds using the submitted details
* The client submits the 3DS code
* The BNN system enters the code received from the client and confirms the receipt of funds

Notes:

* Two ways of obtaining the client's card data are implemented: host-to-host — `h2h` — and payment via a form —
  `payform`. They differ
  only at the first stage, where the order is created and the card data is submitted.
* With `h2h`, the client's card data and the amount are submitted when the order is created.
* With `payform`, the amount is optionally submitted when the order is created; in this case you must redirect the
  client to our form, where they will enter the card data and, if necessary, the amount
* The order information contains a `payFormUrl` field, which holds the address you can direct the client to.
* Once the order processing has finished, the client will be redirected from the payform to the `redirectUrl` address
  specified when the order was created
* With `h2h` the payment form can be used as well, but since the data was submitted when the request was created, the
  client will functionally only be able to enter the 3DS code on the form at the corresponding stage.

The `h2h` flow by status:

```
DETAILS_RECEIVED - the order was created and the user's card data was received in a single request
WAITING_CODE - the system processed the user's card data and is now waiting for the 3ds code from the user
CODE_RECEIVED - the 3ds code was received from the user
SUCCESS - the order was closed successfully
CANCELED - the order was closed UNsuccessfully
```

The `payform` flow by status:

```
CREATED - the order was created
DETAILS_RECEIVED - the system received the user's card data 
WAITING_CODE - the system processed the user's card data and is now waiting for the 3ds code from the user
CODE_RECEIVED - the 3ds code was received from the user
SUCCESS - the order was closed successfully
CANCELED - the order was closed UNsuccessfully
```

### Create an order (h2h flow)

`POST /acquiring`

* On success, information about the order is returned.
* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter          | Type    | Required | Notes                                                                                       |
|--------------------|---------|----------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| externalId         | string  | Yes      | External order key                                                                          |
| amount             | decimal | Yes      | Order amount                                                                                |
| currency           | string  | Yes      | Order currency code                                                                         |
| callbackUrl        | string  | No       | Notification URL                                                                            |
| redirectUrl        | string  | No       | The URL the user is redirected to after paying on the form                                  |
| clientInfo         | string  | No       | Client information (optional information about the client)                                  |
| cardNumber         | string  | Yes      | Card number. Card number validation is enabled — you must submit only a real card number    |
| cardExpirationDate | string  | Yes      | Card expiration date. Format `MM/YY`                                                        |
| cardHolder         | string  | Yes      | Cardholder                                                                                  |
| cardCvv            | string  | Yes      | Card CVV code                                                                               |

Example request:
`/api/acquiring?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Creating an order

```<json>   
{
    "externalId": "37",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "USD",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
    "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
    "clientInfo": "User777",
    "cardNumber": "1234123412341234",
    "cardExpirationDate": "12/25",
    "cardHolder": "IVAN IVANOV",
    "cardCvv": "123"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "externalId": "37",
        "currencyCode": "USD",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "merchantAmount": 95.00,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
        "payFormUrl": "bnn-pay.com/?id=01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "createdAt": "2024-06-28T15:45:02.968357964+03:00",
        "status": "DETAILS_RECEIVED",
        "cardNumber": "123456****1234",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

The response returns information about the order.

* `hash` — the identifier assigned to the order in our system
* `externalId` — the identifier passed by the external system
* `status` — the order status `DETAILS_RECEIVED`
* `cardNumber` — the card number in masked format: the first 6 digits, then `****`, then the last 4 digits (for example `123456****1234`)
* `closeReasonCode` — the order close reason code (filled in when the order is finalised)
* `userCurrency` — the user's settlement currency (filled in for cross-currency orders)
* `exchangeRate` — the factor for converting the user's currency into the order currency
* `secondAmount` — the order amount in the user's currency

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Currency not found in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Валюта не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "An order with external identifier 36 already exists in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка с внешним идентификатором 36 уже существует в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Create an order (payform flow)

`POST /acquiring/cardless`

* The `amount` field is optional.

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter   | Type    | Required | Notes                                                      |
|-------------|---------|----------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| externalId  | string  | Yes      | External order key                                         |
| amount      | decimal | No       | Order amount                                               |
| currency    | string  | Yes      | Order currency code                                        |
| callbackUrl | string  | No       | Notification URL                                           |
| redirectUrl | string  | No       | The URL the user is redirected to after paying on the form |
| clientInfo  | string  | No       | Client information (optional information about the client) |

Example request:
`/api/acquiring/cardless?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Creating an order

```<json>   
{
    "externalId": "37",
    "amount": 100,
    "currency": "USD",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
    "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
    "clientInfo": "User777"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "externalId": "37",
        "currencyCode": "USD",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "merchantAmount": 95.00,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
        "payFormUrl": "bnn-pay.com/?id=01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "createdAt": "2024-06-28T15:45:02.968357964+03:00",
        "status": "CREATED",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

The response returns information about the order.

* `hash` — the identifier assigned to the order in our system
* `externalId` — the identifier passed by the external system
* `status` — the order status `CREATED`

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Currency not found in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Валюта не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "An order with external identifier 36 already exists in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка с внешним идентификатором 36 уже существует в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Submit the user's card data (payform flow) (Data)

`POST /acquiring/send_card_data`

* The `amount` field is conditionally required: it must be submitted if the operation amount (`amount`) was not
  submitted when the order was created via the `POST /acquiring/cardless` method

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter          | Type    | Required | Notes                                                                                    |
|--------------------|---------|----------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| id                 | string  | Yes      | Order hash                                                                               |
| amount             | decimal | No       | Order amount                                                                             |
| cardNumber         | string  | Yes      | Card number. Card number validation is enabled — you must submit only a real card number |
| cardExpirationDate | string  | Yes      | Card expiration date. Format `MM/YY`                                                     |
| cardHolder         | string  | Yes      | Cardholder                                                                               |
| cardCvv            | string  | Yes      | Card CVV code                                                                            |

Example request:
`/api/acquiring/send_card_data?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Submitting card data for a previously created order

```<json>   
{
    "id": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
    "amount": 100,
    "cardNumber": "1234123412341234",
    "cardExpirationDate": "12/25",
    "cardHolder": "IVAN IVANOV",
    "cardCvv": "123"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "externalId": "37",
        "currencyCode": "USD",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "merchantAmount": 95.00,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
        "payFormUrl": "bnn-pay.com/?id=01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "createdAt": "2024-06-28T15:45:02.968357964+03:00",
        "status": "DETAILS_RECEIVED",
        "cardNumber": "123456****1234",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

The response returns information about the order.

* `hash` — the identifier assigned to the order in our system
* `externalId` — the identifier passed by the external system
* `status` — the order status `DETAILS_RECEIVED`

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "The card expiration date has passed":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Дата действия карты истекла"
        }
    ]
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заказ не найден в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Submit the 3ds code for an order

`POST /acquiring/code`

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Request body:

| Parameter | Type   | Required | Notes      |
|-----------|--------|----------|------------|
| hash      | string | Yes      | Order hash |
| code      | string | Yes      | 3ds code   |

Example request:
`/api/acquiring/code?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

```<json>   
{
    "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
    "code": "123456"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```<json>   
{
    "success": true,
    "result": "OK"
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "The order must be in the WAITING_CODE status":

```<json>   
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка должна быть в статусе WAITING_CODE"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get an order by hash

`GET /acquiring/{hash}`

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `hash` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/acquiring/01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "externalId": "37",
        "currencyCode": "USD",
        "amount": 100.0000000000,
        "merchantAmount": 95.0000000000,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
        "payFormUrl": "bnn-pay.com/?id=01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "createdAt": "2024-06-28T12:45:02.968358Z",
        "status": "DETAILS_RECEIVED",
        "cardNumber": "123456****1234",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by hash":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по хэшу не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get an order by external key

`GET /acquiring/ext/{externalId}`

* On success, information about the order is returned.

* Errors may occur while processing. In that case the response has a 4XX code and an error text

* The `externalId` parameter is specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/acquiring/ext/37?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:43:28`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "hash": "01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "externalId": "37",
        "currencyCode": "USD",
        "amount": 100.0000000000,
        "merchantAmount": 95.0000000000,
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/pay/result",
        "redirectUrl": "https://site.com/redirectUrl",
        "payFormUrl": "bnn-pay.com/?id=01dd1a6d-538a-4c4d-bb38-bc128d39f528",
        "clientInfo": "User777",
        "createdAt": "2024-06-28T12:45:02.968358Z",
        "status": "DETAILS_RECEIVED",
        "cardNumber": "123456****1234",
        "closeReasonCode": null,
        "userCurrency": null,
        "exchangeRate": null,
        "secondAmount": null
    }
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Order not found in the system by external ID":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Заявка по внешнему ID не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Get orders by filter

`GET /acquiring`

* All filters are specified in the query parameters
* `timestamp` is the only required parameter
* This method supports pagination. The `pageNumber` and `pageSize` parameters control it
* This method supports sorting together with pagination. The `fieldNameSort` and `isDescSort` parameters control it
* On success, orders are returned in the following format:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "result": {
    "elements": [
      ...
    ],
    "totalElements": 2,
    "totalPages": 1,
    "currentPages": 0
  }
}
```

| Property      | Description                                  |
|---------------|----------------------------------------------|
| elements      | the array of orders described above          |
| totalElements | the total number of elements found           |
| totalPages    | the total number of pages of `pageSize` size |
| currentPages  | the current page                             |

Query parameters

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

| Parameter     | Type     | Required | Notes                                             |
|---------------|----------|----------|---------------------------------------------------|
| timestamp     | DateTime | Yes      | Timestamp                                         |
| hash          | string   | No       | Filter by order hash                              |
| amountMin     | decimal  | No       | Lower amount bound filter                         |
| amountMax     | decimal  | No       | Upper amount bound filter                         |
| currency      | string[] | No       | Filter by currency (several values can be passed) |
| createdAfter  | DateTime | No       | Lower creation date bound filter                  |
| createdBefore | DateTime | No       | Upper creation date bound filter                  |
| orderStatus   | string   | No       | Order status                                      |
| pageNumber    | int      | No       | Page number                                       |
| pageSize      | int      | No       | Page size                                         |
| fieldNameSort | string   | No       | Sort field                                        |
| isDescSort    | boolean  | No       | Sort direction                                    |

`orderStatus` can be multi-valued — you can pass several statuses, for example `SUCCESS` and `CODE_RECEIVED`

Example request:
`/api/acquiring?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:55:11&orderStatus=SUCCESS&pageNumber=1&pageSize=10&fieldNameSort=externalId`

Fetches orders 11 through 20 (`pageNumber=1&pageSize=10`) that are successful (`orderStatus=SUCCESS`), sorted by the
external order key
(`fieldNameSort=externalId`)

-----------------------------------------------------

## Methods for working with disputes

A dispute lets the merchant contest the outcome of an order's processing. Files (screenshots, videos, etc.) can be
attached to a dispute.

The dispute status (`status`) can take the following values:

```
OPEN                     - the dispute was created and is awaiting review
WAITING_FOR_RESPONDENT   - waiting for the respondent (contractor/matcher) to reply
RESOLVED                 - the dispute was resolved in the merchant's favour
REJECTED                 - the dispute was rejected
```

### Create a dispute

`POST /dispute`

* Content-Type: `multipart/form-data`
* The request consists of two parts: `json` (a JSON string with the dispute parameters) and `files` (optional, one or several files)
* To identify the order you must pass at least one of the fields: `hash` or `externalId`

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

Parameters of the `json` part:

| Parameter   | Type          | Required | Notes                                                     |
|-------------|---------------|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| hash        | string (UUID) | No*      | The hash of the related order                             |
| externalId  | string        | No*      | The external identifier of the related order              |
| comment     | string        | No       | The merchant's comment                                    |
| callbackUrl | string        | No       | URL for notifications about dispute status changes        |
| orderType   | string        | Yes      | Order type: `IN`, `OUT`, `ACQ`                            |

*At least one of the `hash` or `externalId` fields must be passed.

Parameters of the `files` part (optional):

* Maximum file size: 15 MB
* Allowed formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, BMP, MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, MPEG

Example request:
`/api/dispute?timestamp=2024-05-04T12:38:25`

#### Creating a dispute (the json part)

```json
{
    "hash": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
    "comment": "The payment was made, but the order was not confirmed",
    "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/dispute/callback",
    "orderType": "IN"
}
```

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "id": "d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
        "orderId": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
        "orderType": "IN",
        "merchantId": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-012345678901",
        "status": "OPEN",
        "comment": "The payment was made, but the order was not confirmed",
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/dispute/callback",
        "files": [
            {
                "id": "f1e2d3c4-b5a6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
                "name": "screenshot.png"
            }
        ],
        "respondentComment": null,
        "respondentFiles": [],
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T12:38:25.123456Z"
    }
}
```

The response returns information about the dispute.

* `id` — the dispute identifier in the system
* `orderId` — the identifier of the related order
* `orderType` — the order type (`IN`, `OUT`, `ACQ`)
* `merchantId` — the merchant identifier
* `status` — the dispute status
* `comment` — the merchant's comment
* `callbackUrl` — the URL for notifications
* `files` — the files attached by the merchant (an array of objects with `id` and `name` fields)
* `respondentComment` — the respondent's comment (filled in after the respondent replies)
* `respondentFiles` — the respondent's files (filled in after the respondent replies)
* `createdAt` — the date and time the dispute was created

Response (status code 4XX) — a dispute for this order already exists; the message reads "A dispute is already in
progress":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": {
        "message": "Апелляция уже в работе",
        "disputeId": "d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
    }
}
```

### Get the list of disputes

`GET /dispute`

* All filters are specified in the query parameters
* `timestamp` is the only required parameter
* Pagination is supported via the `pageNumber` and `pageSize` parameters

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

| Parameter  | Type   | Required | Notes                                        |
|------------|--------|----------|----------------------------------------------|
| timestamp  | string | Yes      | Timestamp                                    |
| status     | string | No       | Filter by dispute status (StatusDispute)     |
| orderType  | string | No       | Filter by order type (`IN`, `OUT`, `ACQ`)    |
| pageNumber | int    | No       | Page number                                  |
| pageSize   | int    | No       | Page size                                    |

Example request:
`/api/dispute?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:55:11&status=OPEN&pageNumber=0&pageSize=10`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "elements": [
            {
                "id": "d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
                "orderId": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
                "orderType": "IN",
                "merchantId": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-012345678901",
                "status": "OPEN",
                "comment": "The payment was made, but the order was not confirmed",
                "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/dispute/callback",
                "files": [],
                "respondentComment": null,
                "respondentFiles": [],
                "createdAt": "2024-05-04T12:38:25.123456Z"
            }
        ],
        "totalElements": 1,
        "totalPages": 1,
        "currentPages": 0
    }
}
```

### Get a dispute by order

`GET /dispute/by_order`

* Returns the dispute related to the specified order
* You must pass at least one of the fields: `hash` or `externalId`

**Parameters:** timestamp in the query string

| Parameter  | Type          | Required | Notes                                        |
|------------|---------------|----------|----------------------------------------------|
| timestamp  | string        | Yes      | Timestamp                                    |
| hash       | string (UUID) | No*      | The hash of the related order                |
| externalId | string        | No*      | The external identifier of the related order |

*At least one of the `hash` or `externalId` fields must be passed.

Example request:
`/api/dispute/by_order?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42&hash=a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "id": "d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
        "orderId": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
        "orderType": "IN",
        "merchantId": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-012345678901",
        "status": "OPEN",
        "comment": "The payment was made, but the order was not confirmed",
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/dispute/callback",
        "files": [],
        "respondentComment": null,
        "respondentFiles": [],
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T12:38:25.123456Z"
    }
}
```

### Get a dispute by identifier

`GET /dispute/{id}`

* The `id` parameter — the dispute identifier (UUID), specified in the request path

Example request:
`/api/dispute/d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200):

```json
{
    "success": true,
    "result": {
        "id": "d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
        "orderId": "a7efd403-7d89-4b3c-9111-b5e3c79528e6",
        "orderType": "IN",
        "merchantId": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-012345678901",
        "status": "WAITING_FOR_RESPONDENT",
        "comment": "The payment was made, but the order was not confirmed",
        "callbackUrl": "https://site.com/dispute/callback",
        "files": [
            {
                "id": "f1e2d3c4-b5a6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
                "name": "screenshot.png"
            }
        ],
        "respondentComment": null,
        "respondentFiles": [],
        "createdAt": "2024-05-04T12:38:25.123456Z"
    }
}
```

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "Dispute not found in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        {
            "errorType": "VALIDATION",
            "message": "Апелляция не найдена в системе"
        }
    ]
}
```

### Download a dispute file

`GET /dispute/{id}/files/{fileId}`

* The `id` parameter — the dispute identifier (UUID)
* The `fileId` parameter — the file identifier (UUID)
* Returns a binary file stream (`application/octet-stream`)

Example request:
`/api/dispute/d1e2f3a4-b5c6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890/files/f1e2d3c4-b5a6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890?timestamp=2024-05-04T13:24:42`

Response (status code 200): a binary file stream

Response (status code 4XX) — the message reads "File not found in the system":

```json
{
    "success": false,
    "error": [
        "Файл не найден в системе"
    ]
}
```
